title: Hatchan Draws His Swords
description: On pages 0–3, hatchan draws his swords focuses on Zoro, Hatchan, Nami, Usopp as zoro fights Hatchan while still badly wounded, proving his will against a powerful fish-man swordsman.
scene: On pages 0–3, hatchan draws his swords focuses on Zoro, Hatchan, Nami, Usopp as zoro fights Hatchan while still badly wounded, proving his will against a powerful fish-man swordsman.

dialogue.speaker: Hatchan
dialogue.text: Don't think your head and 8 limbs will still be in one piece after I'm through with you, octopus!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: What a fun game this'll be!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Hmph, no need to get so worked up.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Even if things hadn't turned out this way, your chances of survival would still be Zoro.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: A Crea- TURE THAT CAN'T EVEN BREATHE UNDER- WATER! HOW PATHETIC!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: This is bad, Johnny! Brother Luffy's in trouble!

dialogue.speaker: Cocoyashi villagers
dialogue.text: He won't be able to do anything in that situation!

summary: This part pushes the Arlong Park battle forward, clarifying who is fighting, what is at stake, and why Nami’s freedom matters.
